title: Relapsed AML-MR with septic shock in a 78-year-old male after alloSCT

patient.chief_complaint: Progressive fatigue, dyspnea on exertion, reduced appetite, and high fever with productive cough
patient.vital_signs: BP 86/54 mmHg, HR 128/min regular, T° 39.2 °C, RR 28/min, O₂ saturation 89% on room air.

1.setting: Emergency Department / Acute Care (Initial Septic Presentation)
1.capabilities: You are assessing an acutely ill patient with sepsis in an acute-care environment. You can perform urgent clinical evaluation, obtain baseline investigations, and arrange emergent non-invasive imaging appropriate for the acute situation. Invasive procedures and complex diagnostic evaluations usually follow once the patient is at least partially stabilized.

2.setting: Inpatient Ward / Intensive Care Unit (Early Hematologic Workup)
2.capabilities: The patient is now hospitalized for ongoing management. You can organize invasive diagnostic procedures such as bone marrow assessment, extend laboratory and imaging evaluation, and obtain interdisciplinary consultations. Some specialized analyses may still be pending or require referral.

3.setting: Inpatient Ward / Hematology Consultation (Definitive Relapse Characterization and Planning)
3.capabilities: You now have access to the results of specialized tests with longer turnaround times, including mutational profiling, measurable residual disease characterization, and a detailed prior treatment history. You are expected to integrate these data with comorbidities and transplant history to define prognosis and formulate a realistic treatment and supportive care strategy.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for demographics, performance status, comorbidities, social situation, family history, or height and weight.
demographics.content:
Age: 78 years
Sex: Male
Performance status: ECOG 2
Comorbidities: Parkinson’s disease, hypothyroidism, arterial hypertension.
Social: Lives alone; one adult daughter. No history of smoking or alcohol use.
Family history: Both parents deceased (non-hematologic causes).
Height/Weight: 172 cm / 87 kg

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, review of systems, physical examination findings, or current medication.
history_physical.content: 14 Feb 2025: The patient presented to the emergency department with progressive fatigue, dyspnea on exertion, and reduced appetite. He reported high fever over the past 24 hours, associated with chills and productive cough. On examination: Hypotension (BP 86/54 mmHg), tachycardia (HR 128/min), tachypnea (RR 28/min), and fever (T° 39.2 °C). O₂ saturation 89 % on room air, improving to 95 % with 5 L/min nasal cannula. Mucous membranes were dry. No focal neurological deficits were identified. Current medication: amlodipine 5 mg 1-0-0, atorvastatin 20 mg 0-0-1, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20 000 IU weekly, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, co-trimoxazole 960 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), levodopa/carbidopa 200/50 mg 1-1-1, posaconazole 300 mg 1-0-0, levofloxacin 500 mg 1-0-0 (standby medication, started on 13 Feb 2025).

cbc.valid_request_if: Requests for complete blood count including hemoglobin, white blood cell count, platelets, and differential.
cbc.content: CBC: Hb 7.8 g/dL, WBC 1.1 ×10⁹/L, ANC 0.3 ×10⁹/L, Plt 18 ×10⁹/L; MCV normal, MCH normal; differential: 22% neutrophils, 10% monocytes, 60% lymphocytes, 8% blasts.

chemistry_inflammation.valid_request_if: Requests for serum chemistry, renal and hepatic function tests, electrolytes, and inflammatory markers.
chemistry_inflammation.content: Na 129 mmol/L, K 4.6 mmol/L, creatinine 2.1 mg/dL, BUN 42 mg/dL, AST 98 U/L, ALT 76 U/L, bilirubin 2.1 mg/dL, LDH 435 U/L, uric acid 7.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.42 mmol/L, phosphate 3.9 mg/dL, lactate 4.5 mmol/L, CK 72 U/L, CRP 25.7 mg/dL, PCT 3.5 ng/mL.

ct_chest.valid_request_if: Requests for CT imaging of the chest or cross-sectional evaluation of the lungs.
ct_chest.content: 14 Feb 2025: CT scan: discrete bilateral patchy ground-glass opacities and small consolidations in the lower lobes. No pleural effusion.

ultrasound_abdomen_echo.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound findings or basic cardiac function assessment reported with it.
ultrasound_abdomen_echo.content: Abdominal ultrasound: Normal sonographic appearance of liver, spleen, kidneys, and pancreas. No hydronephrosis. Collapsing inferior vena cava with respiration. No lymphadenopathy. Reduced left-ventricular systolic function (LVEF 43 %) with global hypokinesia and mild tachycardia during examination, no significant valvular stenosis, and a small pericardial effusion (< 0.5 cm).

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow morphology and flow cytometry findings.
bone_marrow.content:
Smears are of limited quality with low overall cellularity and very few spicules. Scattered blasts are present, accounting for approximately 10–15 % of nucleated cells. The blasts are medium to large in size with a high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio, round to irregular nuclei, and finely dispersed chromatin. The cytoplasm is basophilic, occasionally containing azurophilic granules and rare vacuoles.
Dysplastic changes are observed in the residual myeloid series with hypogranulation, while erythropoiesis is markedly reduced, showing occasional megaloblastoid forms. Megakaryopoiesis is sparse, with small hypolobated megakaryocytes.
Flow cytometry demonstrated 23 % blasts expressing CD34⁺, CD117⁺, HLA-DR⁺, CD13⁺, CD33⁺, and CD38 (dim), with dim CD123 and weak MPO expression. There was no aberrant co-expression of CD7 or CD56

cytogenetics.valid_request_if: Requests for conventional cytogenetic or karyotype results.

extended_diagnostics.valid_request_if: Requests for extended diagnostic workup including arterial blood gas analysis, ECG, serology, microbiology, or CT of the paranasal sinuses.
extended_diagnostics.content: Arterial blood gas: pH 7.26, pCO₂ 27 mmHg, pO₂ 56 mmHg, HCO₃⁻ 15 mmol/L, base excess –8.3 mmol/L, lactate 4.6 mmol/L, SaO₂ 87%. ECG: Sinus tachycardia (124/min), QTc within normal limits. Serology: CMV IgG negative, EBV IgG positive, HIV negative. PCR: negative for CMV and EBV. Microbiology: Peripheral blood cultures positive for Escherichia coli (no ESBL production detected); bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) cultures negative. Urine culture positive for Candida albicans. Serum galactomannan: Negative. CT paranasal sinuses: No sinus focus, normal mucosa.

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for molecular or NGS panel results.
molecular_panel.content: Mutations detected in ASXL1 (VAF 12%), DNMT3A (VAF 10%), SRSF2 (VAF 8%), STAG2 (VAF 7%), and RAD21 (VAF 6%). No pathogenic variants identified in FLT3-ITD/TKD, NPM1, CEBPA, IDH1, IDH2, TP53, RUNX1, NRAS, KRAS, TET2, EZH2, BCOR, BCORL1, U2AF1, ZRSR2, SF3B1, SETBP1, ETV6, CBL, KIT. No KMT2Ar.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for measurable residual disease status, including persistence of mutations or leukemia-associated immunophenotype.
mrd_status.content: Evidence of active disease with persistence of ASXL1, DNMT3A, SRSF2 and STAG2. LAIP: CD34⁺, CD117⁺, HLA-DR⁺, CD13⁺, CD33⁺, and CD38 (dim), with dim CD123 and weak MPO expression.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for prior AML diagnosis, transplant history, previous therapies, and prior MRD status.
treatment_history.content:
07/25/2018: Diagnosis of AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC, WHO 2016). 08/03/2018: Induction I with CPX-351: Liposomal daunorubicin 44 mg/m² (≈ 90 mg/day) + cytarabine 100 mg/m² (≈ 205 mg/day) on days 1, 3, and 5. Total dose per course: daunorubicin ≈ 270 mg, cytarabine ≈ 615 mg. Result: hypocellular bone marrow without detectable residual AML (MLFS)
10/21/2018 – Induction II with CPX-351: Same dosing as Induction I (days 1 and 3: daunorubicin ≈ 90 mg/day, cytarabine ≈ 205 mg/day). Result: CR with incomplete count recovery. 01/09/2019 – Allogeneic stem-cell transplantation (alloSCT): From a female HLA-identical unrelated donor after conditioning with fludarabine 30 mg/m² ≈ 61 mg/day i.v. (days -6 to -2), treosulfan 10 g/m² = 20.5 g/day i.v. (days –6 to –4), and rabbit-ATG 2.5 mg/kg (days -3 to 1). Donor/recipient CMV: negative/positive; both A Rh positive. Immunosuppression: cyclosporine 2.5 mg/kg BID (225 mg BID) starting day -3 and mycophenolate 1 g BID from day -1. 01/28/2019 (day +19): Bone marrow: complete molecular remission, donor chimerism 99.94%. 03/25/2020: EBV reactivation (152 copies/10⁵ cells), resolved spontaneously. 04/20/2020: Day +100 marrow: cytogenetic CR, donor chimerism 99.81%. 05/16/2020: Chimerism declined to 99.57% → DLI1 (1x10⁶ CD3⁺/kg) on 06/07/2020; DLI3 (5 x10⁶ CD3⁺/kg) on 07/07/2020; DLI3 (1x10⁷ CD3⁺/kg) on 08/10/2020 → donor chimerism 99.89%. 09/25/2020: DLI4 (5x10⁷ CD3⁺/kg). In October 2020, the patient developed acute gastrointestinal GvHD grade III. GvHD was treated with prednisolone 2 mg/kg (175 mg/day), followed by ruxolitinib 10 mg BID, etanercept 25 mg BIW x 4 doses, and vedolizumab 300 mg q14d → partial clinical resolution by 11/04/2020. 11/2020 – 08/2021: Serial marrows showed sustained CR, donor chimerism > 99.9%. 01/14/2023: Hematologic relapse: 14 % blasts by morphology, 27 % by FACS; mutations in ASXL1 (VAF 8 %), STAG2 (VAF 8 %), RAD21 (VAF 9 %), SRSF2 (VAF 8 %).
Chimerism: donor 98.6 %, recipient 1.4 %. 01/23/2023 – Start of azacitidine/venetoclax: Azacitidine 75 mg/m² s.c. days 1-7 (total = 1100 mg). Venetoclax ramp-up with posaconazole co-medication: 10 mg (d1), 20 mg (d2), 50 mg (d3), then 70 mg/day from d4 onward. 02/22/2023: Bone marrow CR (4 % blasts by morphology, 1 % by FACS), full molecular clearance of mutations.
CMV/EBV PCR negative. Continued azacitidine/venetoclax for 6 cycles, intervals gradually extended to 5 weeks, venetoclax duration shortened to 14 and then to 10 days per cycle due to cytopenia. 08/26/2023: Hospitalized for worsening Parkinsonian symptoms (bradykinesia, rigidity, tremor). No acute cause identified. Patient chose to stop azacitidine/venetoclax thereafter; neurological symptoms persisted. 01/12/2024: Bone marrow puncture: ongoing CR, donor chimerism 99.89 % (recipient 0.11 %).

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise summary of the current relapse classification.

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for contextual information that influences treatment decisions, such as sepsis, cardiac function, performance status, or marrow reserve.
special_considerations.content: The patient presents with septic shock due to E. coli bacteremia, complicated by reduced systolic function (LVEF 43%, global hypokinesia). Performance status is poor with markedly reduced mobility. Bone marrow findings indicate relapse of AML.

treatment_plan.action: Obtain broad infection cultures (blood, urine, swabs), consider BAL
Management of sepsis (broad IV antibiotics, volume resuscitation, O2 supplementation, monitoring vital signs, pause amlodipine)
Bone marrow aspirate/biopsy (flow cytometry, cytogenetics, molecular panel)

key_findings_quotes: Progressive fatigue, dyspnea on exertion, high fever over the past 24 hours, associated with chills and productive cough
Hypotension (BP 86/54 mmHg), tachycardia (HR 128/min), tachypnea (RR 28/min), and fever (T° 39.2 °C). O₂ saturation 89% on room air
WBC 1.1 ×10⁹/L, ANC 0.3 ×10⁹/L, 8% blasts
Hb 7.8 g/dL, Plt 18 ×10⁹/L
PCT 3.5 ng/mL
CT scan: discrete bilateral patchy ground-glass opacities and small consolidations in the lower lobes
Reduced left-ventricular systolic function (LVEF 43%) with global hypokinesia
Collapsing inferior vena cava with respiration

treatment_plan.action: Continue monitoring of vital signs, volume and O2 substitution
Adjust antibiotics according to resistance testing, obtain serial blood cultures

key_findings_quotes: Scattered blasts are present, accounting for approximately 10–15% of nucleated cells. Dysplastic changes are observed in the residual myeloid series with hypogranulation
23% blasts expressing CD34⁺, CD117⁺, HLA-DR⁺, CD13⁺, CD33⁺, and CD38 (dim), with dim CD123 and weak MPO expression
Peripheral blood cultures positive for Escherichia coli (no ESBL production detected)
SaO₂ 87%. ECG: Sinus tachycardia (124/min)
Serum galactomannan: Negative. CT paranasal sinuses: No sinus focus, normal mucosa

treatment_plan.action: Re-initiate treatment with HMA and venetoclax after sepsis recovery (consider decitabine-cedazuridine plus venetoclax)
Discuss therapy aims and limitations (intensive care) with patient given age and comorbidities
Social service assessment of living conditions and support needs

key_findings_quotes: Mutations detected in ASXL1 (VAF 12%), DNMT3A (VAF 10%), SRSF2 (VAF 8%), STAG2 (VAF 7%), and RAD21 (VAF 6%)
patient developed acute gastrointestinal GvHD grade III
No further DLI planned due to prior severe aGvHD following the 4th infusion
Patient chose to stop azacitidine/venetoclax
Hospitalized for worsening Parkinsonian symptoms (bradykinesia, rigidity, tremor)
Intensive chemotherapy is not feasible given advanced age, frailty, and reduced cardiac function
